content.subtitle: Use
rows: To report what another person said without using their exact words
Lena said that she had finished her homework.

content.subtitle: Form: tense changes
rows:
present simple
'I like pizza,' Tom said.

past simple
Tom said he liked pizza.

present continuous
'We are watching a film,' they said.

past continuous
They said they were watching a film.

present perfect
'I have seen that film,' Anna said.

past perfect
Anna said she had seen that film.

past simple
'I bought a new phone,' Jake said.

past perfect
Jake said he had bought a new phone.

will
'I will help you,' Sarah said.

would
Sarah said she would help me.

can
'I can swim well,' Leo said.

could
Leo said he could swim well.

must
'You must study harder,' the teacher said.

had to
The teacher said we had to study harder.

may
'I may travel abroad,' Nina said.

might
Nina said she might travel abroad.

sections.title: A: Change to reported speech
sections.instructions: Rewrite each sentence in reported speech.

items.sentence: 'I enjoy painting,' Kate said.
'We are cleaning the house,' they said.
items.answer: They said they were cleaning the house.

items.sentence: 'I can ride a horse,' Ben said.
items.answer: Ben said he could ride a horse.

items.sentence: 'They are going to travel abroad,' Mia said.
items.answer: Mia said they were going to travel abroad.

items.sentence: 'I may visit my cousins tomorrow,' Ella said.
items.answer: Ella said she might visit her cousins the next day.

items.sentence: 'I was studying all evening,' Jack said.
items.answer: Jack said he had been studying all evening.

sections.title: B: Complete the reported sentences
sections.instructions: Use the correct reported form of the verb in brackets.

items.sentence: Ryan said he ...... (feel) tired after work.
My friends said they ...... (prepare) dinner then.
Olivia said she ...... (complete) the task already.
Daniel said he ...... (can) help us later.
Mum said we ...... (have to) be careful.
They said they ...... (travel) to Rome the following month.

sections.title: C: Time and place changes
sections.instructions: Rewrite with the correct time/place words.

items.sentence: 'I'll see you tomorrow,' she said.
items.answer: She said she would see me the next day.

items.sentence: 'We arrived here yesterday,' they said.
items.answer: They said they had arrived there the day before.

items.sentence: 'I'm busy now,' Luke said.
items.answer: Luke said he was busy then.

items.sentence: 'I met her last week,' Amy said.
items.answer: Amy said she had met her the week before.

items.sentence: 'I'll stay here tonight,' he said.
items.answer: He said he would stay there that night.

items.sentence: 'I started this job three years ago,' Paul said.
items.answer: Paul said he had started that job three years before.

sections.title: D: Choose the correct option
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: Lucy said she **was / is** hungry.
They said they **can / could** join us later.
He said he **would / will** phone me that evening.
Dad said we **have to / had to** leave early.
Emma said she had seen him **yesterday / the day before**.
Tom said he might visit us **next month / the following month**.

sections.title: E: Correct the errors
sections.instructions: Each sentence has an error in reported speech. Write the whole sentence correctly.

items.sentence: My uncle said he will buy a new car.
items.answer: My uncle said he would buy a new car.

items.sentence: She said they have to study for the test.
items.answer: She said they had to study for the test.

items.sentence: Chris said he can play the piano.
items.answer: Chris said he could play the piano.

items.sentence: Laura said she is working all afternoon.
items.answer: Laura said she had been working all afternoon.

items.sentence: They said they are going to move house.
items.answer: They said they were going to move house.

items.sentence: Sophie said she may call me tomorrow.
items.answer: Sophie said she might call me the next day.

sections.title: F: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct form.

items.sentence: He said he is hungry.
They said they will come later.
items.answer: They said they would come later.

items.sentence: Anna said she can speak Italian.
items.answer: Anna said she could speak Italian.

items.sentence: My teacher said we have to finish the exercise.
items.answer: My teacher said we had to finish the exercise.

items.sentence: She said she would arrive tomorrow.
items.answer: She said she would arrive the next day.

items.sentence: Ben said he has done his homework already.
items.answer: Ben said he had done his homework already.

sections.title: G: Correct the errors
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake in reported speech. Find the bold word and write the correction.

items.sentence: Maria said she **is** tired after school.
They said they **will** help us.
He said he **can** drive very well.
Dad said we **have to** be quiet.
She said she saw him **yesterday**.
Tom said he **has** finished dinner.

sections.title: H: Rephrasing
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so it has a similar meaning to the first. Use between one and four words.

items.sentence: Jack said he ...... later.

items.context: 'We are revising for the test,' they said.
items.sentence: They said they ...... for the test.

items.context: 'I may buy a new laptop next year,' Tina said.
items.sentence: Tina said she ...... a new laptop the following year.

items.context: 'I was reading a book,' Sam said.
items.sentence: Sam said he ...... a book.

items.context: 'I have cleaned my room,' Lily said.
items.sentence: Lily said she ...... her room.

items.context: 'We can meet tomorrow,' my friends said.
items.sentence: My friends said they ...... the next day.
